briefdescription.para: One shot timer to control connection timeout and connection retry.
Flag indicating if LTE is connected or not.

detaileddescription.para: Flag indicating if connect retry is active or not
Array with pointer to the mailboxes of the listener tasks
The connection timeout, if there is no LTE connection established after this specified time (15 minutes) the device shall deactivate LTE modem and switch to sleep mode
If LTE modem is switched off and device is in sleep mode, it shall wake up after this time (24 hours) to try LTE connect again. This functionality is realized by reset of the device.

name
The name of the class
The constructor allocate and register a mailbox and allocates and initialize the one shot timer with duration of 15 min to control connection timeout and connection retry. Finally the flags bLteConnected and bConnectRetry will be set to false.
The destructor is never called and is implemented only for completion reasons. It simply triggers a debug message on info debug level.

Initially the function register the callback functions for PDN events by calling the function pdn_default_ctx_cb_reg from nRF PDN library API and for LTE events by calling function lte_lc_register_handler from nRF public API for the LTE Link Controller. With call of function lte_lc_modem_events_enable the LTE connector enable the modem domain events, which will be handled by LTE link event handler lte_event_handler. LTE module will be initialized and modem will be configured by call of function lte_lc_init and modem's functional mode is set to LTE_LC_FUNC_MODE_ACTIVATE_UICC by calling function lte_lc_func_mode_set, which activate UICC (Universal Integrated Circuit Card) notifications and is required to read IMSI without network connection. Afterwards the function enters the endless loop and wait for messages. Task wakes up when a message is in the mailbox and read the message by calling mailbox read method which returns the pointer pElem to the message. All expected messages will be handled, in case of an unexpected message an error debug message will be triggered. Finally the message will be released by calling class PoolElem method free, if the pointer pElem is not explicitly set to 0.
Message Handling:
eTimerExpired: On timer expiry the pointer pElem will be set to 0, to prevent releasing the message because it is allocated static. Afterwards the function checks if flag bConnectRetry is true and in this case it triggers a reset by call of function sys_reboot. If flag bConnectRetry is not true, the function checks if LTE is still connected (flag bLteConnected is false). In this case the function switch of LTE modem by calling the functions lte_lc_offline, lte_lc_deinit and lte_lc_power_off. Than timer will be sterted with duration of connectRetryTime (24 hours) and flag bConnectRetry is set to true.
eLteConnected: Listeners will be informed by calling member function notify with message of type eLteConnected and flag bLteConnected will be set to true.
eLteDisconnected: Listeners will be informed by calling member function notify with message of type eLteDisconnected and flag bLteConnected will be set to false.
eLteSleepEnter: Listeners will be informed by calling member function notify with message of type eLteSleepEnter.
eLteSleepExit: Listeners will be informed by calling member function notify with message of type eLteSleepExit and a timer will be started with duration connectTimeout (15 min).
eIp6Up: Will be ignored, only debug message at info level will be printed.
eIp6Down: Will be ignored, only debug message at info level will be printed.
eResume: With call of function lte_lc_normal modem will be switched to full functionality. Power Saving Mode will be enabled enabled by calling function lte_lc_psm_req and a LTE connection will be established by calling function lte_lc_connect_async.
eSuspend: Will be ignored, only debug message at info level will be printed

briefdescription.para: Timer expired function for unused cyclic timer.

detaileddescription.para: The function initializes the static pool element e1 with message type eTimerExpired, set LTE connector as sender and put message into own (LTE connector's) mailbox.

listener
Pointer to the mailbox of the listener
The function enter a loop over the maximum number of listeners and if there is no listener registered at index i of array listeners (pointer to mailbox is NULL), it register the listener by copy the pointer to the mailbox in array listeners at index i, before leaving the function.

briefdescription.para: Send message to all registered listeners.

detaileddescription.para:
msg
The message type
The function enter a loop over the maximum number of listeners and if a listener is registered (pointer to listener mailbox in array listeners not NULL) it requests for every registered listener a message from message pool, set message type to parameter msg, set eLteConnector as sender and send message to the listener's mailbox.

briefdescription.para: Timer callback function for unused cyclic timer.

detaileddescription.para:
timer
Pointer to timer of type k_timer
The function retrieve the user-specific data from the timer by calling kernel function k_timer_user_data_get and call LTE connector timer expired function timer1_expired.

briefdescription.para: Send message to its own (LTE connectors) mailbox.

detaileddescription.para:
msg
The message type
The function send a message of type msg to to its own (LTE connectors) mailbox by calling function sendMsg provided by Mbox class.

briefdescription.para: LteConnector LTE link event handler function.

detaileddescription.para:
evt
Pointer to the received LTE link event of type struct lte_lc_evt
The handler function notifies the application of sleep, wakeup and DRX events from LTE modem. These events will be mapped to internal message types and send to LTE connectors own or data manager mailbox before function returns. Mapping is as follows:
LTE_LC_EVT_MODEM_SLEEP_ENTER -> eLteSleepEnter to own mailbox
LTE_LC_EVT_MODEM_SLEEP_EXIT -> eLteSleepExit to own mailbox
all other LTE link events: will be ignored (only debug message) In case of unexpected events the function print an error debug message returns immediatly.

memberdef.argsstring: (uint8_t cid, enum pdn_event event, int reason)
memberdef.name: pdn_event_handler

detaileddescription.para:
cid
The cell ID
event
The received PDN event
reason
error code
The handler function notifies the application of events related to the default PDN context, CID 0. The PDN events will be mapped to internal message types and send to LTE connectors own mailbox before function returns. Mapping is as follows:
PDN_EVENT_CNEC_ESM: will be ignored (only debug message)
PDN_EVENT_ACTIVATED -> eLteConnected
PDN_EVENT_DEACTIVATED -> eLteDisconnected
PDN_EVENT_IPV6_UP -> eIp6Up
PDN_EVENT_IPV6_DOWN -> eIp6Down In case of unexpected events the function print an error debug message returns immediatly

briefdescription.para: LTE connector class derived from task base class.

simplesect.title: Detailed Description
simplesect.para:
LTE connector task handle the connection to the LTE modem, modem wake up and sleep behaviour and inform its listener tasks (power manager and MQTT connector) about these events. It uses the nRF PDN library API and LTE Link API via AT commands to communicate with LTE modem and to receive PDN and LTE Link events. See details in LTE Link API documentation and for PDN library API in PDN library API documentation The LteConnector class provides two handler functions to receive LTE link and PDN events, a method to send messages to its own (LTE connector's) mailbox, which is used by the handler functions to transform external events to internal messages of type PoolElem. It provide a timer callback function and a timer expired function, used to transform external timer expiry event from interrupt context to internal timer expired message of type PoolElem. Furthermore it provide methods to register listener tasks, to notify listener tasks about received external events and to report eDRX values to data manager task. Message Interaction:
Receive
External PDN and LTE Link events
Timer interrupt callback
LTE connector task (self)
Power manager task
Transmit
LTE connector task (self)
Power manager task
MQTT connector task
Data manager task
